Petition For Approval Of Compromise Of Claim Or Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For Minor Or Person With A Disability
Off Calendar Sep-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for Petition of Approval of Compromise of Claim or Action for Minor, Chase Jarratt.

Dept. 505; Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 1:42 p.m. and concluding at 1:47 p.m.(D505)

Kimberly Chu, Esq, specially appearing for and on behalf of Petitioner and Guardian Ad litem Laura Anderson via Video Zoom Conference.
Petitioner and Guardian Ad Litem Laura Anderson not appearing.

Counsel for Petitioner and Guardian Ad Litem presents a brief recitation of the case.

The matter is submitted

The Court finds the settlement is reasonable and it is in the best interest of the claimant.

The court hereby APPROVES Petitioner's Motion for Compromise of Claim for the settlement amount of $24,950.00 minus the following:

Medical Expenses $0
Attorney's Fees $5,906.52
Costs $1,848.84

Balance of settlement in the sum of $17,194.64 shall be deposited in a Blocked Account at Wells Fargo, Branch name: Pyramid Way located at 2221 Pyramid Way, Sparks, NV 89431.

The Petitioner is authorized and directed to execute all documents reasonably necessary to carry out the terms of the settlement.

Proposed Orders Approving Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with a Disability and to Deposit Funds in Blocked Account are signed and filed this date.

Court orders matter continued to December 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for progress report on the deposit of funds in a blocked account.

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar May-03-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for order to apportion and distribute funds by Plaintiff.

Dept. 505; Judge Gail Dekreon; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:10:13 a.m. and concluding at 10:15:40 a.m.

Plaintiff Amanda Jarratt appearing represented by Kimberly Chu, Esq., is placed under oath, and testifies via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Christina Jarratt appearing represented by Kimberly Chu, Esq., is placed under oath, and testifies via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Riley Power Inc. not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff addresses the Court.
AMANDA JARRATT and CHRISTINA JARRATT are examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, Plaintiff's Motion for Order to Apportion and Distribute is GRANTED.

The following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds (both litigation funds and funds from claims against bankrupt asbestos defendant trusts) negotiated after the October 30, 2019, death of DOUGLAS JARRATT in the following proportions:

Estate of DOUGLAS JARRATT, deceased: 0%

AMANDA JARRAT
as Wrongful Death Heir: 25%

CHRISTINA JARRATT
as Wrongful Death Heir: 25%

JESSICA CARIGLIO
as Wrongful Death Heir: 25%

CHASE JARRATT, a minor
as Wrongful Death Heir: 25%

TOTAL: 100%

The signature of AMANDA JARRAT shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate claim and the wrongful death claims with defedantns in the filed asbestos personal-injury-survival and wrongful death action and in those claims made against bankruptcy-related trusts established for asbestos victims.

Proposed order signed as modified. (D505)

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Sep-30-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than October 14, 2021 plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than October 14, 2021 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for December 2, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 3, 2021. Responses to the demand are due no later than December 17, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by January 7, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, January 13, 2022 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Sep-30-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES via CourtCall:
Carmen Sandgren of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (415) 512-4381, appearing for defendant Oscar E. Erickson, Inc.
Vick K. Mansourian of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (213-283-2100), appearing for defendant Riley Power, Inc.
Thomas Steig of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant Chevron USA. Inc.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(b). No opposition filed. Response filed by Designated Defense Counsel.

The trial date is January 18, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is January 28, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.
The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is January 13, 2022. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 31, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is January 14, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 29, 2021. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
